body.title: ScrapsDevice.validate_ticket() spams shared channel on every diagnostic call
body.result: Validated: 28/28 tests pass. Stash work was already committed in pre-reboot commits. No changes needed.
body.description:
Every web 'status update please' ping triggers a batch of 6 scraps channel messages: ScrapsShim.self_test() validates a short ticket (3 messages) and something downstream validates 3 placeholder tickets ('Add thing', 'TODO', 'A meaningful title here') producing 3 more failures — all posted to infra.channel_messages and visible in the shared channel every ~60s.

Root cause: ScrapsDevice.validate_ticket() always calls self._post() for both passes and failures, with no way to suppress channel noise for diagnostic/self-test callers.

Fix: add a `silent: bool = False` parameter to validate_ticket(). When silent=True, skip the self._post() calls entirely. Update ScrapsShim.self_test() to pass silent=True. Identify and fix the caller that validates the 3 placeholder tickets (likely a diagnostic pass in Igor's status-update handler or another shim.self_test()).

**Affected files:** devices/scraps/scraps_device.py, devices/scraps/shim.py, plus whoever is calling validate_ticket with 'Add thing'/'TODO'/'A meaningful title here' (find via grep or channel tracing)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Make validate_ticket() silent by default for diagnostic callers. Do NOT remove channel posting entirely — user-initiated scraps calls should still post. Do not change validation logic.
**Test plan:** Add test: ScrapsDevice().validate_ticket({'title': 'bad'}, silent=True) produces no channel messages. Existing test_scraps_device.py passes unchanged. After deploy: run web 'status update please' and confirm no scraps spam in channel.
